preFix.title: Fitting a carbon monoxide detector
preFix.subtitle: Install a mains-wired or battery-powered CO alarm in the correct location
preFix.excerpt: How to choose the correct position for a carbon monoxide detector relative to boilers, gas appliances, and sleeping areas, and how to fit a mains-wired CO alarm to a ceiling or wall, connect it to a spare fused spur, and test the alarm is functioning correctly.
preFix.sourceNotes: BS EN 50291-1:2018 (domestic carbon monoxide alarms). MHCLG Fire Safety guidance. Gas Safe Register CO alarm installation guidance. Collins Complete DIY Manual.

content.text: Since October 2022, UK law requires a CO alarm in any room with a fixed combustion appliance (including a boiler room). A defective alarm must be repaired or replaced.
alarms contain an electrochemical sensor cell with a life of 7 to 10 years. Check the manufacturer's replacement date label and replace the unit when due, even if the test button still works.

: 1 to 3 metres from each gas appliance, at the same level (ceiling or between 1 and 1.5 m on a wall), in a location where the alarm will be heard from all sleeping areas. Do not mount directly above a gas hob or in a cupboard. On a ceiling, mount at least 300 mm from a wall. On a wall, mount the detector at head height.
Mains-wired alarms on a fused spur are best for new installations. They cannot be silenced by removing a battery and can be

with smoke alarms across the property. Battery alarms with a sealed 10-year battery are acceptable in existing properties where running a cable is not practical. The sensor cell lasts 7 to 10 years in both types. In a sealed-battery alarm, the battery life matches the sensor life.
Step 3, fix the alarm base plate

Hold the alarm base plate at the chosen position and mark the fixing holes with the pencil. Check the plate is level with the spirit level. Drill masonry holes and fit wall plugs for ceiling or solid-wall mounting. On plasterboard, use the supplied plasterboard fixings. Drive the fixing screws securely but do not overtighten: the base plate is plastic and will crack.

For a mains-wired CO alarm, connect to a fused spur (usually a 3-amp fused connection unit) on an existing lighting or ring circuit. Isolate the supply using the live-dead-live procedure before connecting. Connect brown to L, blue to N, and green-yellow earth to E at the connection unit, and brown, blue, and earth from the alarm tail to the corresponding terminals inside the alarm base. Confirm the voltage tester pen shows no voltage before touching the wires.

Clip the alarm head to the base plate. Restore the supply (mains-wired) or install the battery. Wait for the alarm to complete its self-test sequence: a brief chirp indicates the unit is working. Press and hold the test button for 5 seconds to confirm the alarm sounder is functional. Record the date of installation and the sensor replacement date on the label supplied.

Combination smoke and CO alarms are useful where there is only one suitable ceiling position. The ideal position differs for each sensor: smoke sensors work best at the centre of the ceiling, while CO sensors can go anywhere at head height. A combined alarm at ceiling level meets both needs well enough in most rooms.

attrs.fix: Check the display or indicator light for a fault code. Replace the battery if battery-operated. Check the date label — if past the sensor replacement date, replace the unit.
attrs.cause: Low battery; sensor cell at end of service life; fault in the unit.
attrs.symptom: Alarm chirps intermittently without triggering the full alarm

attrs.fix: Move the alarm temporarily to a different room. If the alarm stops, ventilate the room and have all gas appliances checked by a Gas Safe registered engineer. Never assume a nuisance alarm.
attrs.cause: Sensor contaminated by high humidity, cleaning products, or solvents; genuine CO from a faulty appliance.
attrs.symptom: Alarm sounds when there is no apparent CO source

attrs.fix: Replace the battery (battery-operated units) or check the mains supply. If the alarm still does not respond to the test button, replace the unit — a silent CO alarm provides no protection.
attrs.cause: Alarm sounder faulty; unit at end of life; battery depleted.
attrs.symptom: Test button does not trigger the alarm sound

initialFindings.message: PATTERN tutorial has no Method/Steps section and no orderedList — sequential instructions missing
